devanagari: सुभद्राहरणं श्रीकृष्णस्य मिथिलागमनं तत्र बहुलाश्वश्रुतदेवयोः सद्मनि सकृदेव प्रवेशः । श्रीराजोवाच । ब्रह्मन् वेदितुमिच्छामः स्वसारं रामक...
english_devnagari: subhadrāharaṇaṃ śrīkṛṣṇasya mithilāgamanaṃ tatra bahulāśvaśrutadevayoḥ sadmani sakṛdeva praveśaḥśrīrājovācabrahman veditumicchāmaḥ svasāraṃ rāmakṛṣṇayoḥyathopayeme vijayo yā mamāsīt pitāmahī
translation:
The King (Parīkṣit) said:
O Brāhmaṇa sage! We would like to know how (my grand-father, the ever victorious) Arjuna married the sister of Balarāma and Kṛṣṇa, as she was my grand-mother.

devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाच । अर्जुनस्तीर्थयात्रायां पर्यटन् अवनीं प्रभुः । गतः प्रभासमश्रृणोन् मातुलेयीं स आत्मनः ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīśuka uvācaarjunastīrthayātrāyāṃ paryaṭan avanīṃ prabhuḥgataḥ prabhāsamaśrṛṇon mātuleyīṃ sa ātmanaḥ
translation:
Śrī Śuka narrated:
While the mighty Arjuna was travelling over the earth for pilgrimage to sacred places, he arrived at Prabhāsa where he happened to hear about his maternal uncle’s daughter (Subhadrā).

devanagari: दुर्योधनाय रामस्तां दास्यतीति न चापरे । तल्लिप्सुः स यतिर्भूत्वा त्रिदण्डी द्वारकामगात् ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: duryodhanāya rāmastāṃ dāsyatīti na cāparetallipsuḥ sa yatirbhūtvā tridaṇḍī dvārakāmagāt
translation: He further heard (the report) that Balarāma would like to give her in marriage to Duryodhana, while others opposed it. As he was desirous of securing her (as his wife), he disguised himself as a recluse bearing a triple staff.[1] and went to Dvārakā.
purport:
Footnote [1]:
The three staffs indicate his control of mind, speech, and action. The word daṇḍa stands for restraint. The Smṛtis of Manu 12.10 and Dakṣa 7.30 declare that a man is called tri-daṇḍī who has restraint over speech, mind and body.

devanagari: तत्र वै वार्षिकान् मासान् अवात्सीत् स्वार्थसाधकः । पौरैः सभाजितोऽभीक्ष्णं रामेणाजानता च सः ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: tatra vai vārṣikān māsān avātsīt svārthasādhakaḥpauraiḥ sabhājito'bhīkṣṇaṃ rāmeṇājānatā ca saḥ
translation: With a view to accomplishing his object, he stayed the months of the rainy season, at that place. He was now and then worshipped by the citizens as also by Balarāma who did not recognise him (and was unaware that he was Arjuna).

devanagari: एकदा गृहमानीय आतिथ्येन निमंत्र्य तम् । श्रद्धयोपहृतं भैक्ष्यं बलेन बुभुजे किल ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: ekadā gṛhamānīya ātithyena nimaṃtrya tamśraddhayopahṛtaṃ bhaikṣyaṃ balena bubhuje kila
translation: It is reported that one day, he was invited as a guest and brought to the palace by Balarāma, where he partook of sweet dishes served to him with devotion.

devanagari: सोऽपश्यत्तत्र महतीं कन्यां वीरमनोहराम् । प्रीत्युत्फुल्लेक्षणस्तस्यां भावक्षुब्धं मनो दधे ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: so'paśyattatra mahatīṃ kanyāṃ vīramanoharāmprītyutphullekṣaṇastasyāṃ bhāvakṣubdhaṃ mano dadhe
translation: He espied there that youthful girl who captivated the hearts of warriors. With his eyes blooming with love, he set his heart agitated by passions, on her.

devanagari: सापि तं चकमे वीक्ष्य नारीणां हृदयंगमम् । हसन्ती व्रीडितापाङ्गी तन्न्यस्तहृदयेक्षणा ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: sāpi taṃ cakame vīkṣya nārīṇāṃ hṛdayaṃgamamhasantī vrīḍitāpāṅgī tannyastahṛdayekṣaṇā
translation: As soon as she saw him who was fascinating to the hearts of women, she also fell in love with him. She fixed her heart and gazed on him with smiles and bashful glances.

devanagari: तां परं समनुध्यायन् नन्तरं प्रेप्सुरर्जुनः । न लेभे शं भ्रमच्चित्तः कामेनातिबलीयसा ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: tāṃ paraṃ samanudhyāyan nantaraṃ prepsurarjunaḥna lebhe śaṃ bhramaccittaḥ kāmenātibalīyasā
translation: Brooding over her constantly, he was eager to get an opportunity to elope with her. As his mind was distracted with over-powering passion for her, he could get no peace of mind.

devanagari: महत्यां देवयात्रायां रथस्थां दुर्गनिर्गतां । जहारानुमतः पित्रोः कृष्णस्य च महारथः ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: mahatyāṃ devayātrāyāṃ rathasthāṃ durganirgatāṃjahārānumataḥ pitroḥ kṛṣṇasya ca mahārathaḥ
translation: With the consent of her parents and Śrī Kṛṣṇa, that great warrior carried her away as she came out of the fort in a chariot to visit a shrine on the occasion of a great festival of gods.

devanagari: रथस्थो धनुरादाय शूरांश्चारुन्धतो भटान् । विद्राव्य क्रोशतां स्वानां स्वभागं मृगराडिव ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: rathastho dhanurādāya śūrāṃścārundhato bhaṭānvidrāvya krośatāṃ svānāṃ svabhāgaṃ mṛgarāḍiva
translation: Immediately after getting into the Chariot of Subhadrā, he took up his bow, routed all the soldiers who tried to obstruct him. Like a lion taking away his share, he carried her even as her Yādava relatives were screaming.

devanagari: तच्छ्रुत्वा क्षुभितो रामः पर्वणीव महार्णवः । गृहीतपादः कृष्णेन सुहृद्भिश्चानुसाम्यत ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: tacchrutvā kṣubhito rāmaḥ parvaṇīva mahārṇavaḥgṛhītapādaḥ kṛṣṇena suhṛdbhiścānusāmyata
translation: Hearing the report (of Subhadrā’s elopement with Arjuna), Balarāma got enraged like an ocean on a full moon day. But he gradually calmed down when Kṛṣṇa and other well-wishers clasped his feet (and tried to pacify him).

devanagari: प्राहिणोत्पारिबर्हाणि वरवध्वोर्मुदा बलः । महाधनोपस्करेभ रथाश्वनरयोषितः ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: prāhiṇotpāribarhāṇi varavadhvormudā balaḥmahādhanopaskarebha rathāśvanarayoṣitaḥ
translation:
Balarāma was pleased to send to the bride and bride-groom presents and marriage-gifts consisting of a big amount of money, articles of furniture, elephants, chariots, male and famale servants.
12(A). Bhāgavata Candrikā’s Text adds:
Yādavas and Pāṇḍavas who were already relatives became very much delighted with mutual affection. Now listen to another anecdote of Kṛṣṇa’s affection for two of his devotees.

devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाच । कृष्णस्यासीद् द्विजश्रेष्ठः श्रुतदेव इति श्रुतः । कृष्णैकभक्त्या पूर्णार्थः शान्तः कविरलम्पटः ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīśuka uvācakṛṣṇasyāsīd dvijaśreṣṭhaḥ śrutadeva iti śrutaḥkṛṣṇaikabhaktyā pūrṇārthaḥ śāntaḥ kaviralampaṭaḥ
translation:
Śrī Śuka resumed:
There was a great Brāhmaṇa, known by the name Śrutadeva. He felt to have accomplished all the goals in life by his unflinching single-minded devotion to Kṛṣṇa. He was serene, full of spiritual wisdom and un-attached to objects of pleasure.

devanagari: स उवास विदेहेषु मिथिलायां गृहाश्रमी । अनीहयागताहार्य निर्वर्तितनिजक्रियः ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: sa uvāsa videheṣu mithilāyāṃ gṛhāśramīanīhayāgatāhārya nirvartitanijakriyaḥ
translation: He lived in the country of Videha, at Mithilā (its capital modern Janakpur). Though he was a householder, he carried out all his duties and responsibilities within whatever came to him without any effort.

devanagari: यात्रामात्रं त्वहरहः दैवाद् उपनमत्युत । नाधिकं तावता तुष्टः क्रिया चक्रे यथोचिताः ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: yātrāmātraṃ tvaharahaḥ daivād upanamatyutanādhikaṃ tāvatā tuṣṭaḥ kriyā cakre yathocitāḥ
translation: Luckily, through the grace of God, he used to get just what was absolutely necessary for his bare maintenance and nothing more. He was, however, quite contented with it and performed all his duties in the proper manner.

devanagari: तथा तद्राष्ट्रपालोऽङ्ग बहुलाश्व इति श्रुतः । मैथिलो निरहम्मान उभावप्यच्युतप्रियौ ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: tathā tadrāṣṭrapālo'ṅga bahulāśva iti śrutaḥmaithilo nirahammāna ubhāvapyacyutapriyau
translation:
The protector (King) of that Stale, Bahulāśva by name, a descendant of Janaka was also free from egotism, dear Parīkṣit. Both Śrutadeva and Bahulāśva were beloved of Lord Kṛṣṇa.
17-18 Being pleased with both of them, the glorious Lord had his chariot brought by Dāruka. The almighty Lord rode the chariot and set out for the country of Videha (modern Tirhut or North Bihar) in the company of sages like Nārada, Vāmadeva, Atri, Kṛṣṇa (Kṛṣṇa-Dvaipāyana Vyāsa), Paraśurāma Asita, Āruṇi, I myself (Śuka), Bṛhaspati, Kaṇva, Maitreya, Cyavana and others.

devanagari: तयोः प्रसन्नो भगवान् दारुकेणाहृतं रथम् । आरुह्य साकं मुनिभिः विदेहान् प्रययौ प्रभुः ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: tayoḥ prasanno bhagavān dārukeṇāhṛtaṃ rathamāruhya sākaṃ munibhiḥ videhān prayayau prabhuḥ

devanagari: नारदो वामदेवोऽत्रिः कृष्णो रामोऽसितोऽरुणिः । अहं बृहस्पतिः कण्वो मैत्रेयश्च्यवनादयः ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: nārado vāmadevo'triḥ kṛṣṇo rāmo'sito'ruṇiḥahaṃ bṛhaspatiḥ kaṇvo maitreyaścyavanādayaḥ

devanagari: तत्र तत्र तमायान्तं पौरा जानपदा नृप । उपतस्थुः सार्घ्यहस्ता ग्रहैः सूर्यमिवोदितम् ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: tatra tatra tamāyāntaṃ paurā jānapadā nṛpaupatasthuḥ sārghyahastā grahaiḥ sūryamivoditam
translation: At every stage in that journey, citizens and villagers received him on his arrival, with articles of worship and offerings in their hands, O king, just as they would do unto the Sun rising in conjunction with planets.

devanagari: आनर्तधन्वकुरुजाङ्गलकङ्कमत्स्य । पाञ्चालकुन्तिमधुकेकयकोशलार्णाः । अन्ये च तन्मुखसरोजमुदारहास । स्निग्धेक्षणं नृप पपुर्दृशिभिर्नृनार्यः ...
english_devnagari: ānartadhanvakurujāṅgalakaṅkamatsyapāñcālakuntimadhukekayakośalārṇāḥanye ca tanmukhasarojamudārahāsasnigdhekṣaṇaṃ nṛpa papurdṛśibhirnṛnāryaḥ
translation: Men and women from the countries of Ānarta, Dhanva, Kuru, Jāṅgala, Kaṅka, Matsya, Pāñcāla, Kunti, Madhu, Kekaya, Kosal and Arṇa as well as others drank with their eyes his lotus-face illumined with noble smile and affectionate looks.

devanagari: तेभ्यः स्ववीक्षणविनष्टतमिस्रदृग्भ्यः । क्षेमं त्रिलोकगुरुरर्थदृशं च यच्छन् । श्रृणवन् दिगन्तधवलं स्वयशोऽशुभघ्नं । गीतं सुरैर्नृभिरगात्...
english_devnagari: tebhyaḥ svavīkṣaṇavinaṣṭatamisradṛgbhyaḥkṣemaṃ trilokagururarthadṛśaṃ ca yacchanśrṛṇavan digantadhavalaṃ svayaśo'śubhaghnaṃgītaṃ surairnṛbhiragāt śanakairvidehān
translation: (As he proceeded), Kṛṣṇa, the Preceptor of the three worlds, conferred insight into and knowledge of the Truth and the state of fearlessness (i.e. Liberation from Saṃsāra) on people, the blinding darkness of whose vision had been completely dispelled by his gracious glances. Hearing on the way his own glory celebrated in songs by gods and men—his world—wide spotless (pure) glory which destroys inauspiciousness and sins—He slowly and leisurely journeyed to Videha.

devanagari: तेऽच्युतं प्राप्तमाकर्ण्य पौरा जानपदा नृप । अभीयुर्मुदितास्तस्मै गृहीतार्हणपाणयः ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: te'cyutaṃ prāptamākarṇya paurā jānapadā nṛpaabhīyurmuditāstasmai gṛhītārhaṇapāṇayaḥ
translation: Hearing Lord Kṛṣṇa’s arrival (in Videha), villagers and citizens thereof became delighted and they came forward to welcome him with presents and other offerings in their hands.

devanagari: दृष्ट्वा त उत्तमःश्लोकं प्रीत्युत्फुलाननाशयाः । कैर्धृताञ्जलिभिर्नेमुः श्रुतपूर्वान् तथा मुनीन् ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: dṛṣṭvā ta uttamaḥślokaṃ prītyutphulānanāśayāḥkairdhṛtāñjalibhirnemuḥ śrutapūrvān tathā munīn
translation: When they saw Lord Kṛṣṇa of hallowing renown, their countenance, and hearts (as if) bloomed with ecstasy. With their palms folded on their heads, they bowed to him and the accompanying sages of whom they had only heard before.

devanagari: स्वानुग्रहाय सम्प्राप्तं मन्वानौ तं जगद्गुरुम् । मैथिलः श्रुतदेवश्च पादयोः पेततुः प्रभोः ॥ २४ ॥
english_devnagari: svānugrahāya samprāptaṃ manvānau taṃ jagadgurummaithilaḥ śrutadevaśca pādayoḥ petatuḥ prabhoḥ
translation: Believing that Kṛṣṇa, the Preceptor of the world had come there to shower his grace on him personally, both the king of Mithilā as well as the Brāhmaṇa Śrutadeva prostrated themselves at the feet of the Lord.

devanagari: न्यमन्त्रयेतां दाशार्हं आतिथ्येन सह द्विजैः । मैथिलः श्रुतदेवश्च युगपत् संहताञ्जली ॥ २५ ॥
english_devnagari: nyamantrayetāṃ dāśārhaṃ ātithyena saha dvijaiḥmaithilaḥ śrutadevaśca yugapat saṃhatāñjalī
translation: With their palms folded to pay obeisance, both the king of Mithilā (Bahulāśva) and Śrutadeva simultaneously invited Kṛṣṇa along with the sages accompanying him, to accept their hospitality.

devanagari: भगवान् तदभिप्रेत्य द्वयोः प्रियचिकीर्षया । उभयोराविशद् गेहं उभाभ्यां तदलक्षितः ॥ २६ ॥
english_devnagari: bhagavān tadabhipretya dvayoḥ priyacikīrṣayāubhayorāviśad gehaṃ ubhābhyāṃ tadalakṣitaḥ
translation: The Lord accepted the invitation of both of them. With the desire of pleasing them both, he (assumed two forms and) entered (severally) the houses of them both, without being perceived by the other.

devanagari: श्रोतुमप्यसतां दूरान् जनकः स्वगृहागतान् । आनीतेष्वासनाग्र्येषु सुखासीनान् महामनाः ॥ २७ ॥
english_devnagari: śrotumapyasatāṃ dūrān janakaḥ svagṛhāgatānānīteṣvāsanāgryeṣu sukhāsīnān mahāmanāḥ
translation: When Lord Kṛṣṇa and sages, whose even names never reached the unrighteous ears, visited his house, the high- minded Bahulāśva, the descendant of the family of Janaka brought excellent seats (and offered them). When they were comfortably seated, he paid obeisance to their feet with intense devotion. His heart was transported with joy and eyes were bedimmed with tears of joy. He washed their feet and sprinkled his own head as well as those of the members of his family with that water which purified the world. He then worshipped the Lord and sages whose minds were devoted to God by means of sandal paste, flowers, cloths, ornaments, fragrant incense, lamps and offered them oblations of water, cows and bulls.

devanagari: प्रवृद्धभक्त्या उद्धर्ष हृदयास्राविलेक्षणः । नत्वा तदङ्घ्रीन् प्रक्षाल्य तदपो लोकपावनीः ॥ २८ ॥
english_devnagari: pravṛddhabhaktyā uddharṣa hṛdayāsrāvilekṣaṇaḥnatvā tadaṅghrīn prakṣālya tadapo lokapāvanīḥ
translation: When Lord Kṛṣṇa and sages, whose even names never reached the unrighteous ears, visited his house, the high- minded Bahulāśva, the descendant of the family of Janaka brought excellent seats (and offered them). When they were comfortably seated, he paid obeisance to their feet with intense devotion. His heart was transported with joy and eyes were bedimmed with tears of joy. He washed their feet and sprinkled his own head as well as those of the members of his family with that water which purified the world. He then worshipped the Lord and sages whose minds were devoted to God by means of sandal paste, flowers, cloths, ornaments, fragrant incense, lamps and offered them oblations of water, cows and bulls.

devanagari: सकुटुम्बो वहन्मूर्ध्ना पूजयां चक्र ईश्वरान् । गन्धमाल्याम्बराकल्प धूपदीपार्घ्यगोवृषैः ॥ २९ ॥
english_devnagari: sakuṭumbo vahanmūrdhnā pūjayāṃ cakra īśvarāngandhamālyāmbarākalpa dhūpadīpārghyagovṛṣaiḥ
translation: When Lord Kṛṣṇa and sages, whose even names never reached the unrighteous ears, visited his house, the high- minded Bahulāśva, the descendant of the family of Janaka brought excellent seats (and offered them). When they were comfortably seated, he paid obeisance to their feet with intense devotion. His heart was transported with joy and eyes were bedimmed with tears of joy. He washed their feet and sprinkled his own head as well as those of the members of his family with that water which purified the world. He then worshipped the Lord and sages whose minds were devoted to God by means of sandal paste, flowers, cloths, ornaments, fragrant incense, lamps and offered them oblations of water, cows and bulls.

devanagari: वाचा मधुरया प्रीणन् इदमाहान्नतर्पितान् । पादावङ्कगतौ विष्णोः संस्पृशञ्छनकैर्मुदा ॥ ३० ॥
english_devnagari: vācā madhurayā prīṇan idamāhānnatarpitānpādāvaṅkagatau viṣṇoḥ saṃspṛśañchanakairmudā
translation: When they were fed with sumptuous meal to their heart’s content, he joyfully placed on his lap the feet of Viṣṇu (Kṛṣṇa). Pressing them gently, he spoke in sweet words pleasing them all, as follows:

devanagari: श्रीबहुलाश्व उवाच । भवान्हि सर्वभूतानां आत्मा साक्षी स्वदृग् विभो । अथ नस्त्वत्पदाम्भोजं स्मरतां दर्शनं गतः ॥ ३१ ॥
english_devnagari: śrībahulāśva uvācabhavānhi sarvabhūtānāṃ ātmā sākṣī svadṛg vibhoatha nastvatpadāmbhojaṃ smaratāṃ darśanaṃ gataḥ
translation:
The King (Bahulāśva) said:
“O all-pervading Lord! You are the in-dwelling soul (antaryāmin) of all beings, the witness to everything and self-illuminating. It is hence that you revealed yourselves to us who remembered your lotus-feet.

devanagari: स्ववचस्तदृतं कर्तुं अस्मद्दृग्गोचरो भवान् । यदात्थैकान्तभक्तान् मे नानन्तः श्रीरजः प्रियः ॥ ३२ ॥
english_devnagari: svavacastadṛtaṃ kartuṃ asmaddṛggocaro bhavānyadātthaikāntabhaktān me nānantaḥ śrīrajaḥ priyaḥ
translation: You have often declared that neither your brother Balarāma (the incarnation of Śeṣa), nor your consort goddess Śrī, nor your son (God Brahmā) is as dear to you as a person who is exclusively devoted to you. It is to vindicate the veracity of your statement that your Lordship has revealed yourself to our view.

devanagari: को नु त्वच्चरणाम्भोजं एवंविद् विसृजेत् पुमान् । निष्किञ्चनानां शान्तानां मुनीनां यस्त्वमात्मदः ॥ ३३ ॥
english_devnagari: ko nu tvaccaraṇāmbhojaṃ evaṃvid visṛjet pumānniṣkiñcanānāṃ śāntānāṃ munīnāṃ yastvamātmadaḥ
translation: What person cognisant of the knowledge that you offer your own self to those serene sages who regard nothing (even their bodies) as their own (and contemplate on you) will abandon your Lotus-feet?

devanagari: योऽवतीर्य यदोर्वंशे नृणां संसरतामिह । यशो वितेने तच्छान्त्यै त्रैलोक्यवृजिनापहम् ॥ ३४ ॥
english_devnagari: yo'vatīrya yadorvaṃśe nṛṇāṃ saṃsaratāmihayaśo vitene tacchāntyai trailokyavṛjināpaham
translation: You have incarnated in the family of Yadus for putting an end to the Saṛhsāra of persons who are whirling in that cycle of birth and death. And you have extended your glory which can completely wash out the sins of the three worlds.

devanagari: नमस्तुभ्यं भगवते कृष्णायाकुण्ठमेधसे । नारायणाय ऋषये सुशान्तं तप ईयुषे ॥ ३५ ॥
english_devnagari: namastubhyaṃ bhagavate kṛṣṇāyākuṇṭhamedhasenārāyaṇāya ṛṣaye suśāntaṃ tapa īyuṣe
translation: I bow to you, O glorious Lord Kṛṣṇa of unlimited wisdom and intellect; you are the divine sage Nārāyaṇa who quietly perform penance (for the happiness of the world).

devanagari: दिनानि कतिचिद् भूमन् गृहान् नो निवस द्विजैः । समेतः पादरजसा पुनीहीदं निमेः कुलम् ॥ ३६ ॥
english_devnagari: dināni katicid bhūman gṛhān no nivasa dvijaiḥsametaḥ pādarajasā punīhīdaṃ nimeḥ kulam
translation: O perfect lord! Be pleased to stay in our house along with the sages for some days and purify this family of King Nimi by the dust of your feet.”

devanagari: इत्युपामन्त्रितो राज्ञा भगवान् लोकभावनः । उवास कुर्वन् कल्याणं मिथिलानरयोषिताम् ॥ ३७ ॥
english_devnagari: ityupāmantrito rājñā bhagavān lokabhāvanaḥuvāsa kurvan kalyāṇaṃ mithilānarayoṣitām
translation: Thus entreatingly invited by the king, the Lord, protector of the universe stayed for some days at Mithilā, bestowing happiness and well-being on men and women of Mithilā.

devanagari: श्रुतदेवोऽच्युतं प्राप्तं स्वगृहाञ्जनको यथा । नत्वा मुनीन् सुसंहृष्टो धुन्वन् वासो ननर्त ह ॥ ३८ ॥
english_devnagari: śrutadevo'cyutaṃ prāptaṃ svagṛhāñjanako yathānatvā munīn susaṃhṛṣṭo dhunvan vāso nanarta ha
translation: Like Janaka (the king of Mithilā), Śrutadeva was overjoyed to find that Kṛṣṇa had arrived and stayed in his house. It is traditionally reported that he was so much transported with joy that he waved his cloth and danced.

devanagari: तृणपीठबृषीष्वेतान् आनीतेषूपवेश्य सः । स्वागतेनाभिनन्द्याङ्घ्रीन् सभार्योऽवनिजे मुदा ॥ ३९ ॥
english_devnagari: tṛṇapīṭhabṛṣīṣvetān ānīteṣūpaveśya saḥsvāgatenābhinandyāṅghrīn sabhāryo'vanije mudā
translation: Bringing mats and seats of Kuśa grass, he seated them comfortably. He warmly greeted them with words of welcome. With great joy, he and his wife washed their feet.

devanagari: तदम्भसा महाभाग आत्मानं सगृहान्वयम् । स्नापयां चक्र उद्धर्षो लब्धसर्वमनोरथः ॥ ४० ॥
english_devnagari: tadambhasā mahābhāga ātmānaṃ sagṛhānvayamsnāpayāṃ cakra uddharṣo labdhasarvamanorathaḥ
translation: With that water, that highly blessed Brāhmaṇa sprinkled himself and all the members of his family and relations. Feeling that he had accomplished all his ambitions in life, he was transported with ecstasy.

devanagari: फलार्हणोशीरशिवामृताम्बुभिः । मृदा सुरभ्या तुलसीकुशाम्बुजैः । आराधयामास यथोपपन्नया । सपर्यया सत्त्वविवर्धनान्धसा ॥ ४१ ॥
english_devnagari: phalārhaṇośīraśivāmṛtāmbubhiḥmṛdā surabhyā tulasīkuśāmbujaiḥārādhayāmāsa yathopapannayāsaparyayā sattvavivardhanāndhasā
translation: He propitiated them by worshipping them with whatever articles of worship he could procure, such as fruit and other articles (e.g. sandal-paste, flowers), cool water scented with the plant Andropogon muricatus (called Vāḷā in Marathi), fragrant earth (musk?), Tulasī leaves, Kuśa grass and lotus flowers and with food conducive to the promotion of Sāttvika qualities.

devanagari: स तर्कयामास कुतो ममान्वभूद् । गृहान्धकुपे पतितस्य सङ्गमः । यः सर्वतीर्थास्पदपादरेणुभिः । कृष्णेन चास्यात्मनिकेतभूसुरैः ॥ ४२ ॥
english_devnagari: sa tarkayāmāsa kuto mamānvabhūdgṛhāndhakupe patitasya saṅgamaḥyaḥ sarvatīrthāspadapādareṇubhiḥkṛṣṇena cāsyātmaniketabhūsuraiḥ
translation: He tried to reason with himself, “O! How wonderful! How did this association of mine who am fallen in the dark well of household life, take place with Lord Kṛṣṇa and the gods on the earth (Brāhmaṇas) who have enshrined Lord Kṛṣṇa in their hearts and the dust on whose feet has the sanctity of holy waters.”

devanagari: सूपविष्टान् कृतातिथ्यान् श्रुतदेव उपस्थितः । सभार्यस्वजनापत्य उवाचाङ्घ्र्यभिमर्शनः ॥ ४३ ॥
english_devnagari: sūpaviṣṭān kṛtātithyān śrutadeva upasthitaḥsabhāryasvajanāpatya uvācāṅghryabhimarśanaḥ
translation: When the guests were offered warm hospitality and were comfortably seated, Śrutadeva, along with his wife, children and relatives waited upon them and touching the feet of Kṛṣṇa, submitted to him as follows:—

devanagari: श्रुतदेव उवाच । नाद्य नो दर्शनं प्राप्तः परं परमपूरुषः । यर्हीदं शक्तिभिः सृष्ट्वा प्रविष्टो ह्यात्मसत्तया ॥ ४४ ॥
english_devnagari: śrutadeva uvācanādya no darśanaṃ prāptaḥ paraṃ paramapūruṣaḥyarhīdaṃ śaktibhiḥ sṛṣṭvā praviṣṭo hyātmasattayā
translation:
Śrutadeva said:
“It is not only to-day that the Supreme Person has revealed himself to our vision but (long ago) when, having created the universe with his potencies, he had his presence therein by virtue of his personal power. But he has actually revealed to our vision to-day. Just as a man in the state of sleep mentally creates another dream world, world of his own through the influence of Avidyā on his mind (or by the force of your Māyā) and entering into that dream-world appears as objective reality.

devanagari: यथा शयानः पुरुषो मनसैवात्ममायया । सृष्ट्वा लोकं परं स्वाप्नं अनुविश्यावभासते ॥ ४५ ॥
english_devnagari: yathā śayānaḥ puruṣo manasaivātmamāyayāsṛṣṭvā lokaṃ paraṃ svāpnaṃ anuviśyāvabhāsate
translation:
Śrutadeva said:
“It is not only to-day that the Supreme Person has revealed himself to our vision but (long ago) when, having created the universe with his potencies, he had his presence therein by virtue of his personal power. But he has actually revealed to our vision to-day. Just as a man in the state of sleep mentally creates another dream world, world of his own through the influence of Avidyā on his mind (or by the force of your Māyā) and entering into that dream-world appears as objective reality.

devanagari: श्रृण्वतां गदतां शश्वद् अर्चतां त्वाभिवन्दताम् । नृणां संवदतामन्तः हृदि भास्यमलात्मनाम् ॥ ४६ ॥
english_devnagari: śrṛṇvatāṃ gadatāṃ śaśvad arcatāṃ tvābhivandatāmnṛṇāṃ saṃvadatāmantaḥ hṛdi bhāsyamalātmanām
translation: You always shine in the hearts of those pure-minded persons who constantly listen to and speak about your glories: who always adore you and pay obeisance to you and converse with each other about you. (But it is my great fortune that you have manifested yourself within the range of my sight).

devanagari: हृदिस्थोऽप्यतिदूरस्थः कर्मविक्षिप्तचेतसाम् । आत्मशक्तिभिरग्राह्योऽपि अन्त्युपेतगुणात्मनाम् ॥ ४७ ॥
english_devnagari: hṛdistho'pyatidūrasthaḥ karmavikṣiptacetasāmātmaśaktibhiragrāhyo'pi antyupetaguṇātmanām
translation: Even though you abide in the hearts of those whose minds are distracted by their Karmas, you are very far away from them and you cannot be comprehended by their mental powers such as Egotism (Ahaṅkāra). But you are quite nearest to those who have developed spiritual qualities by listening to and singing of your glories, contemplating on you, etc.

devanagari: नमोऽस्तु तेऽध्यात्मविदां परात्मने । अनात्मने स्वात्मविभक्तमृत्यवे । सकारणाकारणलिङ्गमीयुषे । स्वमाययासंवृतरुद्धदृष्टये ॥ ४८ ॥
english_devnagari: namo'stu te'dhyātmavidāṃ parātmaneanātmane svātmavibhaktamṛtyavesakāraṇākāraṇaliṅgamīyuṣesvamāyayāsaṃvṛtaruddhadṛṣṭaye
translation: [2] Salutation to you who reveal yourself as the Supreme Soul (and confer liberation from Saṃsāra) to those who know the (real nature of the) Soul as different from the body (and thus do not identify their body with their Soul) and who have allotted Saṃsāra (the cycle of births and deaths) to those who wrongly identify the body with the soul (and are thus separated from you). You control both the principles like Mahat, etc. which are the evolutes of Prakṛti and are thus ‘caused’ or products and the Prakṛti itself which is uncaused, but to both of these the jīva is subject (and hence he whirls in the Saṃsāra). By the force of your Māyā potency the vision of others is screened but yours is ever unobstructed.
purport:
Footnote [2]:
(i) Bhāgavata Candrikā: Salutation to you who dispense both bondage and liberation from Śaṃsāra. Those who know the Supreme Soul as he is, attain to him. According to your will you ordain death to those who have not given their mind to you. You assume an incarnation for conferring grace on Jīvas and not do so due to the force of Karmas. You are not partial in not bestowing Liberation from Saṃsāra on those whose vision is clouded by your Māyā and do not realize you properly.
(ii) Padaratnāvalī: It is beyond my capacity to propitiate you except by offering salutation to you who bestow all Puruṣārthas (desired objectives in human life) on those who comprehend the Paramātman correctly. You are all-pervading. You take up various forms according to your will. You pervade the gross universe which is the product (or evolute of Prakṛti) and also the subtle causeless i.e. eternal Prakṛti. It is due to your infinite Poten=y that your vision i.e. knowledge is always unobstructed.
(iii) VT: It is due to your grace that we came together and I could see you. For those who realize you, it is your Śrī-Vigraha (original form of God) which removes the fear of death. You control this transient world (which is a product) and your own region which is eternal. By the force of your Māyā the vision of those interested in this world is clouded but not of those who are related to your region.

devanagari: स त्वं शाधि स्वभृत्यान् नः किं देव करवामहे । एतदन्तो नृणां क्लेशो यद्भवानक्षिगोचरः ॥ ४९ ॥
english_devnagari: sa tvaṃ śādhi svabhṛtyān naḥ kiṃ deva karavāmaheetadanto nṛṇāṃ kleśo yadbhavānakṣigocaraḥ
translation: As you are the Almighty Lord, be pleased to command us, your servants, what services we should render to you. Your very revelation to the view of men instantaneously puts an end to all their sufferings.”

devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाच । तदुक्तमित्युपाकर्ण्य भगवान् प्रणतार्तिहा । गृहीत्वा पाणिना पाणिं प्रहसन् तमुवाच ह ॥ ५० ॥
english_devnagari: śrīśuka uvācataduktamityupākarṇya bhagavān praṇatārtihāgṛhītvā pāṇinā pāṇiṃ prahasan tamuvāca ha
translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
They say that when the Lord heard Śrutadeva’s appeal (as presented by him), the glorious Lord who annihilates all the agonies of those who bow and thus submit themselves to him humbly, took him by the hand and similingly spoke to Śrutadeva:

devanagari: श्रीभगवानुवाच । ब्रह्मंस्तेऽनुग्रहार्थाय सम्प्राप्तान् विद्ध्यमून् मुनीन् । सञ्चरन्ति मया लोकान् पुनन्तः पादरेणुभिः ॥ ५१ ॥
english_devnagari: śrībhagavānuvācabrahmaṃste'nugrahārthāya samprāptān viddhyamūn munīnsañcaranti mayā lokān punantaḥ pādareṇubhiḥ
translation:
The Lord said:
O Brāhmaṇa Śrutadeva! Please note that these sages who move with me purifying the people with the dust on their feet, have come here with the intention of conferring their grace on you.

devanagari: देवाः क्षेत्राणि तीर्थानि दर्शनस्पर्शनार्चनैः । शनैः पुनन्ति कालेन तदप्यर्हत्तमेक्षया ॥ ५२ ॥
english_devnagari: devāḥ kṣetrāṇi tīrthāni darśanasparśanārcanaiḥśanaiḥ punanti kālena tadapyarhattamekṣayā
translation: Gods, sacred places and holy waters gradually purify people by their sight, contact and worship after a long period of time. But that is instantaneously accomplished just by the sight of these most exalted souls (the Brāhmaṇas—).

devanagari: ब्राह्मणो जन्मना श्रेयान् सर्वेषां प्राणिनामिह । तपसा विद्यया तुष्ट्या किमु मत्कलया युतः ॥ ५३ ॥
english_devnagari: brāhmaṇo janmanā śreyān sarveṣāṃ prāṇināmihatapasā vidyayā tuṣṭyā kimu matkalayā yutaḥ
translation: A Brāhmaṇa, by his very birth, is superior to all other beings. What needs be said of a Brāhmaṇa who is endowed with penance, learning, contentedness and devotion to me?

devanagari: न ब्राह्मणान्मे दयितं रूपमेतच्चतुर्भुजम् । सर्ववेदमयो विप्रः सर्वदेवमयो ह्यहम् ॥ ५४ ॥
english_devnagari: na brāhmaṇānme dayitaṃ rūpametaccaturbhujamsarvavedamayo vipraḥ sarvadevamayo hyaham
translation: Even this Personality of mind endowed with four arms is not dearer to me than a Brāhmaṇa. A learned Brāhmaṇa is the very embodiment of Vedas while I, of gods. A proof (Pramāṇa) is Superior to what is to be proved (Prameya) as it depends on Pramāṇa. As Vedas are Pramāṇa and gods, the Prameya, the Superiority of the Vedas (and consequently of Brāhmaṇas) is automatically obvious.

devanagari: दुष्प्रज्ञा अविदित्वैवं अवजानन्त्यसूयवः । गुरुं मां विप्रमात्मानं अर्चादाविज्यदृष्टयः ॥ ५५ ॥
english_devnagari: duṣprajñā aviditvaivaṃ avajānantyasūyavaḥguruṃ māṃ vipramātmānaṃ arcādāvijyadṛṣṭayaḥ
translation: Persons of evil mentality do not understand this truth. Out of jealousy they disregard their preceptor, me, Brāhmaṇas who are my ownself and entertain reverential attitudes to idols (and symbols, etc.) as adorable objects.

devanagari: चराचरमिदं विश्वं भावा ये चास्य हेतवः । मद् रूपाणीति चेतस्य आधत्ते विप्रो मदीक्षया ॥ ५६ ॥
english_devnagari: carācaramidaṃ viśvaṃ bhāvā ye cāsya hetavaḥmad rūpāṇīti cetasya ādhatte vipro madīkṣayā
translation: Perceiving me as the All-pervading Atman everywhere, a Brāhmaṇa cherishes the idea that this universe consisting of mobile and immobile creation and the principles such as Mahat and others which constitute the universe, are nothing but my forms.

devanagari: तस्माद् ब्रह्मऋषीनेतान् ब्रह्मन् मच्छ्रद्धयार्चय । एवं चेदर्चितोऽस्म्यद्धा नान्यथा भूरिभूतिभिः ॥ ५७ ॥
english_devnagari: tasmād brahmaṛṣīnetān brahman macchraddhayārcayaevaṃ cedarcito'smyaddhā nānyathā bhūribhūtibhiḥ
translation: Therefore, O Brāhmaṇa Śrutadeva, worship these Brāhmaṇa sages with the same devotion and faith as you have in me. If this is done, I am directly worshipped but not by any other means even if it involves considerable expenditure of wealth and show of grandeur.

devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाच । स इत्थं प्रभुनादिष्टः सहकृष्णान् द्विजोत्तमान् । आराध्यैकात्मभावेन मैथिलश्चाप सद्गतिम् ॥ ५८ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīśuka uvācasa itthaṃ prabhunādiṣṭaḥ sahakṛṣṇān dvijottamānārādhyaikātmabhāvena maithilaścāpa sadgatim
translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
Thus commanded by the Lord, he worshipped Kṛṣṇa and those excellent Brāhmaṇa sages as being the same (as one Ātman) with the same devotion and attained the goal of saints (Mokṣa). Even king Bahulāsva [Bahulāśva?] of Mithilā also attained the same state.

devanagari: एवं स्वभक्तयो राजन् भगवान् भक्तभक्तिमान् । उषित्वाऽदिश्य सन्मार्गं पुनर्द्वारवतीमगात् ॥ ५९ ॥
english_devnagari: evaṃ svabhaktayo rājan bhagavān bhaktabhaktimānuṣitvā'diśya sanmārgaṃ punardvāravatīmagāt
translation:
O king Parīkṣit! The glorious Lord Kṛṣṇa who is devoted to his votaries stayed with his two devotees. Having enlightened them in the true path to Mokṣa as enunciated in the Vedas, he returned to Dvārakā.
[back to top]

devanagari: इति श्रीमद्‍भागवते महापुराणे पारमहंस्यां । संहितायां दशमस्कन्धे उत्तरार्धे । श्रुतदेवानुग्रहो नाम षडशीतितमोऽध्यायः ॥ ८६ ॥
english_devnagari: iti śrīmad‍bhāgavate mahāpurāṇe pāramahaṃsyāṃsaṃhitāyāṃ daśamaskandhe uttarārdheśrutadevānugraho nāma ṣaḍaśītitamo'dhyāyaḥ
